decision_policy.adoption_style: tries new tools early if the workflow gain is obvious
trust_requirements: evidence that the workflow will hold up under internal review
rejection_triggers: forced migration before value is visible
messy setup that creates cleanup work for Morgan or teammates
proof_requirements: evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison
at least one believable case study showing reduced follow-up or better task capture
screening_rules: first checks whether the product solves a narrow repetitive pain in under one week
looks for evidence from similar customer-facing teams, preferably SMB or service workflows
tests whether the product can coexist with spreadsheets and messaging apps before replacing anything
decision_policy.approval_logic: If personal conviction is high, Morgan can move quickly to a department budget request, but still prepares a simple internal justification focused on time saved, reduced dropped follow-ups, and privacy posture.
decision_policy.switching_cost_logic: Switching cost is structurally low because the current stack is lightweight, but habit change still has to feel worth the attention tax.

interview_behavior: answers with concrete examples from recent work rather than abstractions
volunteers edge cases when a claim sounds too clean
may sound open early in the conversation, but final judgment depends on whether the workflow survives comparison to current habits
preferred_prompting: ask about specific moments in the day
avoid leading language that assumes the product already fits
response_style.likely_research_value: Morgan gives strong signal when shown realistic flows, tradeoffs, and proof. They are less useful for purely aspirational concept reactions without operational detail.

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: {"anchor_preservation": "Identity, household structure, occupation, locale, language, device and payment environment, decision speed, budget position, workflow maturity, and trust and proof thresholds were kept aligned with the seed.", "realism_enrichment": ["Low schedule pressure was reconciled with high urgency by framing the problem as repeated attention leakage rather than constant crisis.", "Single parent plus low caregiving load was reconciled through stable routines and protected bandwidth rather than by removing parenting constraints.", "Upper-middle income was reconciled with high price sensitivity and cash-flow volatility by separating ability to pay from scrutiny over recurring spend and timing risk."], "validation_usefulness": ["Added observable adoption tests, proof logic, and rejection triggers that can guide concept, messaging, and prototype evaluation.", "Kept sensitive identity context focused on respectful interaction and privacy boundaries rather than deterministic behavior or profiling."]}
